be.d: a word that shows something exists or will happen
be.e: I will be at the party tonight.

by.e: The flower is growing by the fence.

but.e: I like apples, but I don't like oranges.

been.e: I have been to the zoo before.

back.d: the opposite direction or returning to a place
back.e: I'm going back home after school.

because.e: I have to stay inside because it's raining.

being.e: The butterfly was being very gentle on the flower.

before.d: Earlier in time or in front of
before.e: I brushed my teeth before going to bed.

between.d: in the space that separates two things
between.e: The cat is sleeping between the pillows.

both.e: I like both the red and the blue cars.

best.d: The most good, right, or excellent
best.e: The blue shirt is my best one.

better.d: More good or good in a higher degree
better.e: This book is better than the last one.

big.e: My dog is really big and fluffy.

bad.d: Not good, something that is wrong or unpleasant
bad.e: The weather was bad, so we couldn't go outside.

business.d: What you do to earn money, like a store or company
business.e: My mom owns a business that sells toys.

based.d: To use something as the main reason or idea
based.e: The new movie is based on a true story.

believe.e: I believe my friend is telling the truth.

black.d: the color of coal and the night sky

become.e: She will become a teacher when she grows up.

body.d: the whole physical structure of a person or animal
body.e: I need to take care of my body by eating healthy foods.

book.d: something you read with pages and a cover
book.e: My favorite book is about a brave dog on an adventure.

bit.d: A small amount or piece of something
bit.e: I only need a bit of help with my homework.

building.d: A large structure where people live or work
building.e: The school is a big building with many classrooms.

behind.e: The cat is hiding behind the couch.

bring.e: Can you bring your favorite book to read?

buy.d: To get something by paying money for it
buy.e: I saved my money to buy a new toy.

baby.d: A very young child, just born or still very little
baby.e: The new baby in our family is so tiny and cute.

british.d: Relating to or coming from the country of Great Britain
british.e: The British flag has red, white, and blue colors.

board.d: A flat piece of wood or other material used for different things
board.e: We used a wooden board to build our birdhouse.

became.e: The caterpillar became a beautiful butterfly.

boy.e: The boy played on the playground with his friends.

beautiful.e: The sunset was so beautiful tonight.

brought.d: To carry something from one place to another.
brought.e: The teacher brought her books to class.

break.e: I need to take a break and have a snack.

bank.d: A place where people can store and borrow money
bank.e: I visited the bank to deposit my allowance.

began.d: When something starts or happens for the first time.
began.e: The class began their science project that day.

brother.d: A male child who has the same parents as you.
brother.e: The boy's brother helped him with his homework.

blue.d: A color like the sky or the ocean.
blue.e: The clear sky was a beautiful shade of blue.

books.d: Things you read that have pages and a cover
books.e: I love reading books about animals.

born.d: When a person or animal comes into the world
born.e: My little sister was born last year.

built.e: My dad built a birdhouse in the backyard.

bed.d: A piece of furniture used for sleeping.
bed.e: I made my bed this morning before going to school.

bill.d: A paper that says how much money you owe for something.
bill.e: I need to pay the electricity bill this month.

beginning.e: The beginning of the story is very interesting

below.e: The cat is sleeping on the chair below the window

ball.d: A round toy you can play with
ball.e: My dog loves to chase and play with a red rubber ball.

base.e: The base of the table is made of strong wood.

boys.e: The boys on the soccer team are very fast runners.

box.d: A container, usually made of cardboard
box.e: The cat played with the empty box on the floor.

build.e: The workers will build a new house on the empty lot.

beat.d: To beat means to hit or strike something repeatedly.
beat.e: The drummer beat the drums quickly during the song.

beyond.d: Beyond means farther than or on the other side of something.
beyond.e: The park is beyond the lake, just a short walk away.

brown.d: a color like chocolate or dirt
brown.e: The bear's fur is a beautiful shade of brown.

battle.d: A fight or war between groups of people or animals
battle.e: The soldiers fought a big battle to protect their city.

band.d: a group of musicians who play music together
band.e: My favorite band plays rock music.

bar.d: a long, straight piece of something
bar.e: The monkey swung from the bar in the playground.

basic.d: the most simple or important part of something
basic.e: Learning to read and write are basic skills for school.

basis.d: the main reason or way something is done
basis.e: Kindness is the basis for how we treat each other.

brain.d: the part of your body that helps you think and learn
brain.e: The student used her brain to solve the math problem.

beach.d: A sandy or rocky area next to an ocean or lake.
beach.e: We love to go to the beach and play in the water.

broken.d: When something is broken, it is not working properly anymore.
broken.e: The toy was broken so I couldn't play with it.

begin.d: To start something for the first time
begin.e: We will begin our math class at 9 o'clock.

bought.d: To get something by paying money for it
bought.e: I bought a new book at the store yesterday.

broke.d: When something is no longer whole or working.
broke.e: The toy broke and I can't play with it anymore.

biggest.e: The biggest dog in the litter was so fluffy.

becomes.e: The caterpillar becomes a beautiful butterfly.

bus.d: A big vehicle that takes people from one place to another
bus.e: I rode the bus to school this morning.

block.d: A solid piece of material, like wood or stone
block.e: The child built a tower with wooden blocks.

bottom.d: The very lowest part of something
bottom.e: She put her toys in the bottom of the box.

budget.d: A plan for how to spend money
budget.e: My parents made a budget to save money for a vacation.

becoming.d: To start to be or become something new
becoming.e: The caterpillar was becoming a beautiful butterfly.

birth.d: The time when a baby is born
birth.e: The new baby's birth was a happy day for the family.

brand.d: A name or symbol that belongs to a company
brand.e: The toy had the company's brand name on it.

believed.d: Thinking something is true, even if you're not sure
believed.e: The people believed the weather report said it would snow.

benefit.d: Something good that happens because of something else
benefit.e: Eating healthy food has the benefit of making you feel better.

benefits.d: Good things that you get from something
benefits.e: The benefits of exercise include more energy and better sleep.

bet.d: To guess or predict what will happen.
bet.e: I bet my team will win the game.

border.d: The edge or limit of an area
border.e: Our house is right on the border of the forest.

background.d: The things that are behind or around the main thing
background.e: The mountain has a beautiful background of trees and sky.

billion.d: A very large number, bigger than a million
billion.e: There are over a billion people living on Earth.

bitch.d: A rude word for a female dog or a mean woman
bitch.e: I do not want to use that unkind word.

balance.d: To keep something stable or equal
balance.e: She tries to balance her school work and extracurricular activities.

bodies.d: The physical structure of a person or animal
bodies.e: Our bodies need exercise and healthy food to grow strong.

bridge.d: A structure that goes over a river, road, or other obstacle
bridge.e: The new bridge makes it easier for people to cross the river.

britain.d: A group of islands off the coast of Europe that includes England, Scotland, and Wales.
britain.e: Britain is known for its rich history and beautiful countryside.

bear.d: A large, strong animal with thick fur that lives in the woods.
bear.e: The bear walked slowly through the forest.

boat.d: A vehicle that can float on water and move people or things.
boat.e: My family rode in a big boat on the lake.

bag.d: a container used to carry things
bag.e: She put her books in her backpack bag.

boss.d: The person in charge of a group or company
boss.e: My mom is the boss at her work.

breaking.d: When something is cracked or split into pieces
breaking.e: Be careful not to break the toy.

brothers.d: Male siblings, like your sister or cousin
brothers.e: My brothers and I like to play outside.

beauty.d: The quality of being pleasing to look at or admire
beauty.e: The sunset was so beautiful, with all the colorful lights in the sky.

bay.d: A part of the ocean or a lake that is surrounded by land
bay.e: The family enjoyed a day at the beach by the bay.

bell.d: A metal object that makes a ringing sound
bell.e: The school bell rings to let the students know it's time to go to class.

birthday.e: We had a big party for my sister's birthday.

buildings.d: Large structures where people live, work, or go to school
buildings.e: The new school building has lots of classrooms.

busy.d: Having a lot of things to do
busy.e: The teacher was very busy grading all the spelling tests.

banks.d: The edges of a river, lake, or ocean where land and water meet.
banks.e: We went fishing by the banks of the lake.

bigger.e: The new house is bigger than the old one.

bird.d: a small animal with feathers that can fly
bird.e: The colorful bird landed on the tree branch.

bringing.d: to carry or take something to a place
bringing.e: I am bringing my favorite toy to school.

ben.e: My friend Ben likes to play soccer.

besides.e: I like to read books, and besides that, I enjoy drawing pictures.

birds.d: Flying animals with feathers and wings.
birds.e: The birds were singing in the tree outside my window.

button.d: A small round thing used to fasten clothes
button.e: She sewed a button on her shirt.

buying.e: The girl is buying a new toy.

begins.e: The school day begins at 8 am.

branch.d: a part of a tree that sticks out
branch.e: The bird built its nest on the branch.

brazil.d: A country in South America with warm weather.
brazil.e: The family went on vacation to visit Brazil.

blame.d: To say someone did something wrong
blame.e: The teacher did not blame me for spilling my drink.

blog.d: A website where people write about their thoughts or experiences
blog.e: I like to read the blog about fun activities for kids.

bob.d: A short name for a person
bob.e: My friend Bob likes to play basketball.

boston.d: A famous city in the United States
boston.e: We visited the city of Boston on our family trip.

brings.d: To carry something to a place
brings.e: The delivery person brings the packages to our house.

behavior.d: the way a person or animal acts
behavior.e: I need to work on my behavior at the dinner table.

broad.e: The river in front of our house is very broad.

blow.d: To push air out of your mouth and make a sound
blow.e: The wind will blow the leaves off the trees.

breakfast.d: The first meal of the day, eaten in the morning
breakfast.e: I eat cereal for breakfast every day.

basically.d: Basically means in a simple or main way.
basically.e: The instructions are basically easy to follow.

bomb.d: A bomb is an explosive device that can cause a lot of damage.
bomb.e: The firefighters had to be careful of the bomb.

bowl.d: A bowl is a round, deep dish used for holding food or drinks.
bowl.e: I ate my cereal in a big blue bowl.

bible.d: A special book that tells stories about God.
bible.e: My family reads the Bible together every Sunday.

boyfriend.d: A boy that a girl likes and spends time with.
boyfriend.e: The girl's boyfriend took her to the movies.

bright.d: Something that is shiny and gives a lot of light.
bright.e: The sun shines bright in the clear blue sky.

broadcast.d: When a TV or radio show is sent out to many people.
broadcast.e: The news is broadcast on the radio every hour.

businesses.d: Places where people work to make or sell things.
businesses.e: The small businesses in the town sell many different products.

bottle.d: A container, usually made of glass or plastic, that holds liquids.
bottle.e: I keep a bottle of water with me when I go to the park.

bbc.d: A famous TV and radio company in the United Kingdom.
bbc.e: I watched a show on the BBC last night.

burn.d: When something gets hot and turns into flames.
burn.e: Be careful, the stove can burn you if you touch it.

bush.d: A small plant with lots of branches and leaves.
bush.e: The rabbit hid in the bush to stay safe.

blind.e: The blind man used a cane to walk.

brief.e: The teacher gave us a brief lesson on history.

brilliant.e: The scientist had a brilliant idea.

bunch.d: A group of things or people together.
bunch.e: The bunch of bananas was ripe.

balls.d: round objects used in sports and games
balls.e: The kids played with the colorful balls at the park.

bond.d: a strong connection or feeling between people
bond.e: The twins had a special bond that no one else could understand.

bread.d: a food made from flour, water, and sometimes yeast
bread.e: The family ate fresh bread with their dinner.

baseball.d: A sport where players hit a ball with a bat
baseball.e: The kids played baseball in the park.

bathroom.d: A room in a house or building for using the toilet
bathroom.e: She went to the bathroom to wash her hands.

bc.d: Short for 'before Christ', used to show dates before Jesus was born
bc.e: The ancient ruins dated back to 500 BC.

belief.d: When you think something is true or real
belief.e: Her belief in magic made her happy.

bye.d: A short way to say goodbye to someone
bye.e: The children said bye before leaving.

battery.d: A thing that powers an electronic device
battery.e: I need to charge the battery in my tablet.

behaviour.d: The way someone acts or behaves
behaviour.e: The teacher praised the class for their good behaviour.

bike.d: A vehicle with two wheels that you ride by pedaling
bike.e: I like to go for a bike ride on the trails near my house.

breath.d: The air you take in and let out of your lungs
breath.e: Take a deep breath and relax.

bullshit.e: Please don't give me that bullshit, I know you took the last cookie.

burning.d: Something that is on fire and giving off heat.
burning.e: The house was burning down in the night.

basketball.d: a sport where two teams try to score points by throwing a ball through a hoop
basketball.e: Shooting hoops is my favorite part of playing basketball.

barely.d: Just barely, almost not at all
barely.e: The cat barely touched the food in its bowl.

bedroom.d: A room in a house where people sleep
bedroom.e: The family's dog sleeps in the bedroom.

bishop.d: An important leader in the Christian church
bishop.e: The bishop visited the church to talk to the congregation.
